summary: Selenocysteine is recognized as a standard amino acid in modern biochemistry due to its incorporation into proteins via specific translational machinery involving unique RNA elements and tRNAs. Its inclusion reflects both structural distinction (with selenium) and functional role within protein synthesis pathways.
